message:
fix(wecom): track token cache state in health check and improve manifest

Store actual token expiry state instead of hardcoded false in health
check. Add test to verify health check reflects cached token status.
Update manifest description from bot to app for accuracy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -6,13 +6,15 @@ schema_version = "2.1"
 id = "fcp.wecom"
 name = "WeCom Enterprise Messaging"
 version = "0.1.0"
-description = "Tenant-scoped WeCom connector for enterprise bot messaging, media upload, directory lookups, and health verification"
+description = "Tenant-scoped WeCom connector for enterprise app messaging, media upload, directory lookups, and health verification"
 archetype = "operational"
 homepage = "https://developer.work.weixin.qq.com/"
 
 # Auth and scope model:
 # - One connector instance is bound to one WeCom tenant application.
 # - Authentication uses `corp_id`, `agent_id`, and `agent_secret`.
+# - The operator provisions that application out of band and grants the app the
+#   tenant permissions needed for outbound messaging and directory reads.
 # - The connector acts as the configured enterprise application; user impersonation,
 #   callback verification, and inbound webhook or websocket delivery are explicit non-goals
 #   for this first slice.
